query: before I start coding this new rate-limiter, what principles should I keep in mind so I don't overbuild it?
remind me of the anti-LLM-mistake coding guardrails

what are the coding principles we follow in this repo? the karpathy ones
how should I approach this code change so I define success criteria up front instead of just hacking until it looks done

give me a checklist to think before coding — assumptions, alternatives, that kind of thing
what guardrails keep me from touching unrelated code when I'm only supposed to fix one function?
im starting a feature and want to make sure i ask clarifying questions before writing anything — what's the principle for that
we have a parser module and a validator module that both walk the AST. someone wants to merge them into one. should we?
the team is split on rewriting the job scheduler from scratch vs refactoring it incrementally. what framework should we use to decide?
this change would rename every occurrence of 'session' to 'context' across 40 files — is that the right approach or should we keep them separate?
we keep killing the idea of unifying the deploy skill and the release skill, but it comes up every sprint. how should we evaluate it this time?
I need to decide whether to make this config change schema-level (hard to undo) or feature-flag-level (easy to revert). what's the principle?
implement the new rate-limiter in src/middleware/ratelimit.ts with a sliding window and add tests
refactor the auth module to extract the token-validation logic into its own file and update all callers
review this PR for correctness bugs and tell me what to fix
the login test fails intermittently with a timeout — help me find the root cause
this paragraph in my SKILL.md is too wordy, compress it without losing any technical facts
fix the off-by-one in the pagination offset, it's showing duplicate rows on page 2
what does the SOLID acronym stand for in object-oriented design?
run the test suite and tell me which tests are currently failing on main
what's the difference between a rebase and a merge in git?

should the variable be named 'retryCount' or 'numRetries'?
should we add a --dry-run flag to this CLI command?
explain how consistent hashing works and when you'd use it
